messages.content:
Savey said on 01 December 2023

It appears the BBC One Christmas idents this year are focusing on CBBC show KikoRiki, Good considering the characters in that show are anthropomorphic animals, making them awesome for this time of year, I'd assume they will launch tonight in the junction between Doctor Who and Unbreakable, Doctor Who is their highest rating show, so it makes sense to lead into Unbreakable by launching the Christmas idents, Or they could hold it off till tommorow and launch them at Strictly. Whatever works.

Currently still running with the normal dancers idents (when will they be retired, they've been used since 2002)

Emma said on 01 December 2023

Hopefully S4C does something with their new blob mascot, Last year they had TV sets stacked up on top of each other, Then for 10 months they had flickering TV sets, They have finally rebranded and have introduced their first ever mascot: a shape-shifting blob, I hope BBC One rebrand soon, those dancers sure are getting tired, As for the KikoRiki themed Christmas idents, I was told they are launching tonight after Doctor Who, They are being introduced just before Unbreakable.

HouseofFun said on 01 December 2023

Doctor Who ending now, announcement playing "A team of 8 people are pushed to the absolute limit, Unbreakable, our brand new game show is up next, But first, Time to say hello and spend Christmas with the Doctor" I'm not liking this one a whole lot..., They seem to be running a trailer advertising the Doctor Who Christmas special, which revolves around the Doctor having to protect his companions from a man who seeks to find a fob watch (the one The Doctor made himself human with one time), Although just before Doctor Who tonight they had a announcement saying "Christmas is switched on tonight in 1 hour in time for Unbreakable, But first, Is this the end for the Doctor?". Doesn't seem like they are advertising the Christmas pres at all, 

Edit: Christmas pres featuring KikoRiki switched on, First ident shown features Rosa the Pig doing a dramatic ice skate dance (She even uses eyedrops for fake tears) While this is happening we hear dramatic music, The announcer says "Welcome to Christmas on BBC One, Let us entertain you, But now a team of 8 people are being pushed to their absolute limits, Are they Unbreakable?" Not sure why they didn't tell us it was happening untill it did, But still a gorgeous ident, I noticed when the Announcer started talking it sounded as if he was driven to tears by the beauty of Rosa's gorgeous dance. The best thing to come out of this package since the "Lights" christmas idents in 2006, 

Eyes said on 01 December 2023

A beautiful ident, Best thing to come out of the "Rythm and Movement" package since those Planet Earth 3 special idents a few months ago, i feel sad that KikoRiki has not been shown much on CBBC on BBC Two, but I'm glad it's representing the Xmas idents on BBC One (Although I also wish the idents also featured a merman).

Maile said on 01 December 2023

Best Christmas ident in a while, I just love that they have spent it with Rosa and her friends, I hear there is also one featuring Krash the Rabbit opening his presents, I like how they used the usual ident theme (dancing) in that first ident, KikoRiki looks like it's gonna be good this year, but I hope they do more with the style (and maybe finally retire Rythm and Movement idents)

Faramad said on 01 December 2023

News safe ident just on, Basically just a Christmas tree on a snowy background, with a music box Christmas carol playing, on top of that the only thing we see is a sleeping Rosa (We vaguely hear her snoring) Glad they are now using characters in their news safe idents, Announcer said "Now on BBC One, time for the BBC News at 8". Remember the Lights idents the used just before overhauling their promo style in late 2006/early 2007? That was pretty cool, but this is way better. I seem to remember those idents started on a dark street but Christmas lights started turning on before it got to a city center and then people were dancing, It was used again in 2007, And now it seems like the "Project Chameleon" endboard template introduced in July is out the window, at least for now, replaced with a snowy background with a Christmas tree on it. Signs that Rythm and Movement is finally working towards the end.

KikoRiki said on 01 December 2023

We're glad you love our cartoon and the special BBC One idents featuring our characters, 

However, Rosa will be the star, (We will have idents revolving around Chiko, Krash and the others), As to what you said, BBC One are looking into introducing the Circle idents already in use in other countries, so don't expect to see Ballet, Capoeria or Festival/Rave back in the new year. Get used to Kites, Capes, Hippos and Bikes as that's what might be coming after our Christmas campaign, We don't know for certain, but the Dancers might not be coming back after Christmas

WhovianForever: Did everyone catch the Doctor Who Christmas special announcement during the news? I’m so hyped! Can’t wait to see how the Doctor deals with the fob watch saga this holiday season!

LemonyFan: And how about they’re showing the 2004 ‘A Series of Unfortunate Events’ movie!! That’s one of my holiday favorites; the nostalgia is real. 😍🍿

ChristmasCarol: Truly is shaping up to be a wonderful holiday schedule on BBC One. Between Doctor Who and the classic movies, I’m going to be glued to my screen with all the mulled wine and mince pies!

KikoFanatic: Plus, the KikoRiki Christmas idents have been adorable so far! Rosa’s ice skate dance nearly brought tears to my eyes just like the announcer. 🐷⛸ Christmas is definitely switched on!

RetroTV: I’m just here wondering if the new ‘Circle’ idents KikoRiki hinted at is a sign we’re finally saying goodbye to the “Rythm and Movement” package for good. After 20+ years, it’s about time for a major refresh!

NostalgiaNerd: @RetroTV I’m with you on that. The Lights idents back in '06/'07 were beautiful. It’s cool to see they’re still willing to shake things up visually with the news safe ident this year. Sleepy Rosa might just be my new favorite. 😴🎄

SeriesBinger: Doctor Who special + A Series of Unfortunate Events + KikoRiki idents = Best Christmas TV lineup or what? Also, hope BBC One throws in some classic Christmas episodes of our favorite shows. Maybe some ‘Vicar of Dibley’ or ‘Only Fools and Horses’?

CircleCurious: Anyone heard more about these new Circle idents? I love change, and KikoRiki characters have been great, but I’m already excited for what’s coming next. Bring on the new era!

KikoLover: Just saw a new Krash and Chiko ident! They’re having a blast playing in the snow! ❄️🐇 So cute watching Krash throw snowballs while Chiko dodges around. Really captures the winter fun vibes.

SciFiSavvy: Speaking of new idents, did anyone else notice the Dokko the Moose building a laser device ident? It aired right before ‘Doctor Who’ and set the PERFECT tone for sci-fi. Dokko’s always been the tech genius of the group. 🦌🔧

HedgehogAdmirer: @KikoLover Chiko is always adorable, but seeing him in the snow was next level! BBC One knows how to do Christmas idents right. Plus, the animation on these characters in the idents is super impressive this year.

WhovianGuru: @SciFiSavvy Loved the Dokko ident! It’s like a mini episode of ‘Doctor Who’ in itself. Brilliant tie-in to the show’s theme! Makes waiting for the Christmas special even more exciting.

BakeMaster: But guys, let’s talk about the Olga the Owl baking ident. The detail on that gingerbread house she was making! I can almost smell the cookies through the screen. 🦉🍪 It’s the cozy ident I never knew I needed in my life.

HolidayWatcher: Right?! The Olga ident feels like being wrapped in a warm blanket with a hot chocolate in hand. It’s chef’s kiss perfection. BBC One’s going all out with these KikoRiki characters for Christmas.

DoctorFan: Dokko’s ident was like a mini teaser for what’s coming with ‘Doctor Who’. Immersive and full of that tech mystery vibe. I appreciate that BBC One creates these unique intros for thematic continuity.

AnimationAppreciator: Major kudos to the animation team for the KikoRiki idents. As someone who dabbles in animation, the quality is sensational. It’s like a little holiday gift before each show starts!

ChristmasCheer: Can’t get enough of the snow play with Krash and Chiko. It’s absolutely delightful and spreads so much joy. These little moments provided by the idents add a special touch to the holiday season.

SciCompanion: Dokko and his inventions always remind me that anything’s possible with a little imagination. Such an inspiring ident to lead into ‘Doctor Who’. Well played, BBC One! Can’t wait to see what else is in store, I'm gonna miss these idents when new year comes around! 😢

WelcomeToSciFi: The Dokko centric ident really sets the scene for what's to come on ‘Doctor Who’ this Christmas. One of the best things about the KikoRiki set, Far better than the usual dancers, Come to think of it, they have been using the dancers since 2002, so now is a good time for a revamp, I guess 😉

Olgo5: Just saw the Olga ident and damn, Olga really is a talented chef, They always seem to show it before cooking shows, Which ident normally leads into Eastenders, It seems to vary between Krash and Chiko in the snow, Olga cooking and Rosa ice skating. Not sure how this is consistent, But that Rosa one brings  tears to my eyes with her graceful skating, Not sure why the Dokko one only plays before Doctor Who, Maybe because it fits it well

Prolouge:

User1: So, what’s everyone thinking BBC One’s going to pull out of the hat for this year’s Christmas idents? Last year was decent, but I’m hoping for something with a bit more pizzazz.

WhovianForever: I’m crossing my fingers for something Doctor Who-themed! With David Tennant back in the show earlier this year, it would be perfect timing to capitalize on that for the festive season. Can you imagine an ident with the TARDIS all decked out in Christmas lights?

HolidayJoy: That would be cool for us Whovians, for sure, but BBC One usually goes for something that appeals to everyone. Doctor Who is big, but not universally loved by every single viewer. Plus, they tend to lean towards more generic festive symbols.

SeriesBinger: True, they’ll probably aim for something more inclusive. Like a cozy winter scene or playful animation. Maybe bring back some classic characters or even partner with a popular children’s show this time around for wider appeal?

RetroTV: I’ve got a hunch they’ll go with animation this year. It has that universal charm, right? Plus, it would work perfectly with both the kids’ content and the family-friendly vibe they love during Christmas.

User1: That’s an interesting angle. They could get quite creative with animation. Plus, they’re probably due for a change since the “Rythm and Movement” dancers have been around since 2002.

KikoFanatic: What if they teamed up with a show like KikoRiki? The characters are fun, visually engaging, and it’s something that would stand out. Plus, they could do loads with those quirky little animals.

LemonyFan: KikoRiki would be such a cute choice! It could be a hit with children and adults alike if done right. And it’s refreshingly different from usual idents, without relying on something niche like a specific Doctor Who theme.

WhovianForever: Okay, I see your point. Much as I’d love Doctor Who idents, something like KikoRiki could offer that ‘Christmas with the family’ vibe they probably want. I just hope they add a little extra magic this year, you know?

CircleCurious: Whatever they do, I hope it’s something new. We’ve had the “Rythm and Movement” package idents for such a long time. It’s high time for something fresh and exciting to take us into the new year! Write a follow up asking a user what the heck ITV are doing in terms of presentation,

User1: With BBC One really stepping up their game with the KikoRiki Christmas idents, it's got me curious. What the heck is ITV doing in terms of presentation this year? Any word on their holiday plans or are they sticking with the usual fare?

Write a convo about what ITV are doing, also what Channel 4 and S4C are doing (Channel 4 are doing a special Snowman ident still using the normal theme of "parts of the 4 logo forming up then dispersing")

**TellyFan1**: Has anyone caught wind of what ITV's plans are for their Christmas presentation this year? After seeing what BBC One has rolled out with the KikoRiki idents, I'm keen to know how they're responding.

**BroadcastBuddy**: Saw a sneak peek of ITV's lineup. They’re weaving a cozy, winter wonderland theme across their idents this year. Think classic Christmas, but with a modern twist. Not as bold as BBC One, but definitely aiming for that warm, family vibe.

**ChannelChaser**: Meanwhile, Channel 4 is sticking to their guns with the iconic Snowman but incorporating the "parts of the 4 logo forming up then dispersing" theme. It's a nod to their classic style, with the Snowman bringing in that festive touch. Probably banking on nostalgia to pull viewers in.

**WelshWatcher**: Don’t forget about S4C! After last year's flickering TV sets and the introduction of their new blob mascot, they're getting creative. From what I've heard, they're planning something magical with the blob transforming into seasonal icons and winter scenes. Sounds like they're really embracing their new mascot in fun ways.

**TellyFan1**: ITV's approach sounds safe but nice. I was hoping they might shake things up a bit more, though. Channel 4's Snowman ident seems like a sweet balance between tradition and brand identity. 

**BroadcastBuddy**: True, ITV might be playing it safe, but their holiday specials will likely be the real draw. And with Channel 4, they always manage to keep that quirky edge, even with something as classic as the Snowman.

**ChannelChaser**: Yeah, Channel 4 knows how to stir up feelings without changing too much. It's smart branding. Keeps the continuity while still being seasonal.

**WelshWatcher**: S4C's initiative really intrigues me, though. It feels like they're the wildcard here, potentially offering the most visually interesting and dynamic presentation. If done right, that blob could become a memorable part of their holiday identity.

**TellyFan1**: It's fascinating to see how each channel has its own strategy for the holidays. BBC One going all in with an animated series theme, ITV embracing the classic holiday spirit, Channel 4 striking a balance with nostalgia, and S4C getting creative with their mascot.

**BroadcastBuddy**: Definitely makes channel-hopping more interesting during the holidays! Each channel is offering something unique. It'll be cool to compare them as we get deeper into the festive season.

**WelshWatcher**: Agreed. It's a showdown of festive spirits. But in the end, it’s the viewers who win with such a variety of holiday visuals and specials to enjoy.

**ChannelChaser**: Can't wait to see them all in action. Here's to a cozy holiday season filled with great TV! 📺🎄
Write a follow up asking what's happening on Jan 1, because promos on Jan 3 still use the snowy tree/Krash and Chiko making snow angels endboard template,

**TellyFan1**: Hey everyone, now that we're past the big holiday season, I've noticed something intriguing on BBC One. Despite it being well into January, the promos airing on Jan 3 are still using the snowy tree/Krash and Chiko making snow angels endboard template. Any ideas on what's happening? Are we seeing an extended holiday theme this year?

**BroadcastBuddy**: It's quite unusual for them to extend the holiday visuals into January. Typically, channels are quick to switch back to their standard branding. However, it could be a strategic move to keep the festive spirit going a bit longer, given how well-received the KikoRiki campaign has been.

**ChannelChaser**: I thought I was the only one who spotted that! It's definitely out of the ordinary. Maybe there's been a delay in rolling out their new branding for 2024, or they've decided to stretch the holiday cheer to counteract the typical January blues.

**WelshWatcher**: On S4C, they've already switched back to their usual ident package, including the blob mascot, since the beginning of January. Seems like BBC One is taking a unique path this year. Maybe it's their way of slowly transitioning out of the festive season?

**TellyFan1**: Interesting points! It does make the cold January days a bit more bearable to still see some festive remnants on TV. I just wonder when they plan to fully transition to their regular programming idents. It's a bit of a mixed message but a pleasant one nonetheless.

**BroadcastBuddy**: There might be a broader strategy at play. Keeping the holiday idents running a bit longer could be a test to see how audiences respond to extended festive content, potentially influencing future decisions on seasonal branding duration.

**ChannelChaser**: Agreed. Plus, it’s a clever way to keep conversations like ours going about their brand well into the new year. Seems like BBC One is playing the long game with viewer engagement this season.

**WelshWatcher**: Could also be a practical aspect to it, perhaps aligning with a new campaign or programming launch they've got planned for later in January. Extending the holiday idents might just be a way to bridge the gap until they're ready to unveil what's next.

**TellyFan1**: Those are all valid points. It’ll be interesting to see how this strategy plays out and what BBC One has up their sleeve for the rest of January. Hopefully, they'll surprise us with something exciting. Let’s keep our eyes peeled for any changes or announcements in the coming days!
Write a follow up where we learn the day it's to happen,

**TellyFan1**: Guys, I got an update! Just saw an announcement that BBC One is planning to unveil their new branding and idents package on January 15th. Looks like the extended use of the holiday idents was indeed a bridge to something new. What does everyone think they have planned?

**BroadcastBuddy**: Finally, some clarity! January 15th is an interesting choice, well after the usual New Year refreshes we’re used to. I’m curious if they’re going with a totally new theme or building upon elements from the holiday idents, considering how popular those were.

**ChannelChaser**: That’s great news! I'm hoping for something innovative that shakes things up. BBC One has a history of strong visual storytelling with their idents. Maybe they’ll incorporate some tech-forward or interactive elements this time around?

**WelshWatcher**: I wonder how this will compare to what S4C and the other channels are doing. S4C often aligns closely with BBC in terms of timing for new launches. Perhaps this could signal a wider refresh across several channels?

**TellyFan1**: It’ll be interesting to see if this new package will set the tone for branding strategies across the board. Especially curious about whether the themes from the KikoRiki idents over Christmas will carry over or if it was just a festive one-off.

**BroadcastBuddy**: With the date set, it gives us something to look forward to. I'm hoping the BBC will drop some teasers before the launch. It’d be great to get a sneak peek at the direction they’re heading in.

**ChannelChaser**: Definitely keeping an eye out for any leaks or teaser promos. They’ve been pretty tight-lipped about it so far, but I bet there’s going to be at least a little something released to pique our interest before the big reveal.

**WelshWatcher**: Absolutely, a teaser or two would do wonders to build anticipation. I’m also keen to see how this new branding will be reflected across their digital platforms. It’s always fascinating to watch a major channel undergo a significant transformation.

**TellyFan1**: Mark your calendars for January 15th, everyone! It sounds like we’re in for a treat. Let’s reconvene once the new package is out. I’m excited to hear everyone’s thoughts and see how BBC One aims to innovate this time around.
Write a follow up on that day, with Bikes as the first new ident at 6am, also we say the last ident at 3am beforehand

**TellyFan1**: It’s the day, everyone! Woke up early just to catch the debut of BBC One's new idents package. The first one aired at 6AM and, you guessed it, it featured Bikes! It's a refreshing change. The whole sequence was dynamic, showcasing various people riding through beautifully lit, diverse landscapes. Really gives off a vibe of moving forward and connectivity. And for those who missed it, the last of the holiday idents aired at 3AM - a bit of a sentimental moment saying goodbye to the festive themes.

**BroadcastBuddy**: Caught the Bikes ident this morning and was impressed by the cinematography! It’s a huge departure from the holiday idents but in a good way. It felt very contemporary, and the music was uplifting too. A promising start to the new package!

**ChannelChaser**: I loved the Bikes ident! It was so vibrant and full of life, exactly what you want to kick start a new year. It symbolizes energy, diversity, and progression. If this is the direction BBC One is taking with their branding, count me in for the ride.

**WelshWatcher**: The transition from the holiday idents to this new theme feels like a breath of fresh air. The Bikes ident had a sense of freedom and exploration to it. Also appreciated the inclusivity with the range of people and locations featured. It's exciting to think about what other themes will accompany Bikes in the lineup.

**TellyFan1**: Agreed! It's a bold move to start with such an energetic and optimistic theme like Bikes. It sets a high bar for the rest of the ident series. I'm curious to see how each new ident will build upon this initial theme while bringing its own unique flair to the table.

**BroadcastBuddy**: One thing’s for sure, BBC One is aiming to make a statement with these new idents. Moving away from the festive KikoRiki theme to something as universal yet engaging as Bikes shows they’re thinking about wide appeal and storytelling through simple, relatable moments.

**ChannelChaser**: It’s fascinating to think about the thought process behind choosing Bikes as the launch ident. It’s universally relatable yet forward-looking. Very clever of BBC One to balance heritage with innovation. Can't wait to see the rest of the idents they've lined up.

**WelshWatcher**: Absolutely. And considering this first glimpse, the production quality suggests we're in for some visually stunning presentations ahead. I hope they maintain this level of creativity and engagement with the audience throughout the entire series.

**TellyFan1**: With the Bikes ident setting such a positive and energetic tone for 2023, I'm eager to see how BBC One will continue to surprise us. It’s a fantastic start to the year. Let's stay tuned for what's next and reconvene here to share thoughts. Cheers to a new era on BBC One!
Write a convo wondering if there was any send off for the usual dancers "despite them having been removed on Dec 1 after DW"

**TellyFan1**: Does anyone else feel like we missed a proper send-off for the usual dancers? They've been a staple of BBC One's identity for so long, and then, just like that, they were gone after Doctor Who on December 1st.

**RetroWatcher**: Absolutely! You'd think for idents that have been around for over two decades, there would be some sort of farewell special or at least a nod to their legacy. It felt a bit abrupt just moving on without acknowledging the end of an era.

**DanceLover**: I was really hoping for a final montage or something celebrating the many years we've had those idents. I mean, they've been such an iconic part of BBC One's visual signature. It's sad to see them go without much fanfare.

**VisualNostalgic**: Exactly, some retrospective would have been great. Remember all the different themes they had through the years? A look back at their evolution would have been a fitting tribute.

**BroadcastBuddy**: To be fair, perhaps BBC One wanted to focus more on the future and the excitement of bringing in something new? Still, I agree that recognizing the significance of what's being left behind is important for long-time viewers.

**ChannelChaser**: That's a valid point. The transition to the new Bikes ident and the forthcoming series represents a fresh direction. But even a small acknowledgment during the transition period would have meant a lot to those of us who've grown attached to the dancers.

**TellyFan1**: Maybe they thought the dancers had become so background to people that a big send-off wasn't necessary? But clearly, based on our conversation, there's a lot of love and nostalgia for them.

**RetroWatcher**: It's a reminder of how quickly things change in the world of broadcasting. What's familiar one day can be history the next. Still, it would have been nice for BBC One to acknowledge the connection viewers had to the idents.

**DanceLover**: Maybe we can hope for a retrospective feature online or as part of a documentary down the line? There's definitely enough interest and affection for the dancers to warrant a deeper dive into their history and impact.

**VisualNostalgic**: Let's keep our fingers crossed for that. It would be a wonderful way to celebrate a visual element that, in its own unique way, was a significant part of British pop culture.

**TellyFan1**: For now, though, we'll just have to cherish our memories of the dancers and look forward to creating new connections with the upcoming idents. Here's to embracing change and honoring the past!

Write the final announcement at 3am, last ident was sleeping Rosa, the announcer tells us "If you're expecting your dancer troupe back at 6am then there's some bad news, we're getting a fresh new look then" before bidding goodnight to us and to Rosa

In the quiet, early hours of the morning, as the last ident of a sleeping Rosa gracefully fades out, the calm and reflective voice of the announcer fills the airwaves, marking the end of an era and the dawn of a new chapter for BBC One.

"Good evening, everyone. As we watch Rosa rest peacefully under the twinkling stars of this serene winter night, it's a moment to reflect and cherish the memories we've shared. If you're among those staying up late or just tuning in, hoping to greet your familiar dancer troupe at 6am, then I'm afraid I have some news. We're turning a page and stepping into a fresh new look when the clock strikes six. A vibrant start awaits us all, with promises of new stories to tell and new memories to create together."

The announcer's voice carries a mixture of nostalgia and anticipation, a gentle reminder of the constant evolution that shapes our experiences and memories, whether it's on the screen or in the world around us.

"So, as we bid goodnight to you and to Rosa, let's look forward to the morning with open hearts and curious minds. Embrace the change, and who knows? You might just find a new favourite to fall in love with. Goodnight, take care, and see you on the other side of this exciting new beginning."

With that, the screen transitions into the standard overnight placeholder, leaving viewers with a sense of closure and curiosity for what the dawn will bring. It's an intimate farewell to an iconic part of BBC One's visual identity, promising a fresh and vibrant future while honoring the legacy left behind.
Write a forum convo describing the placeholder

**ScreenSavvy**: Hey everyone! Did anyone else stay up to catch the final moments of the old BBC One idents last night? After the sleeping Rosa ident, there was an interesting transition. Instead of jumping straight to the usual test card, they used a unique placeholder until the new idents premiered at 6am. It was pretty minimalist but still caught my eye. 

**PixelPusher**: Yeah, I saw that! It was a classy touch. Rather than the standard color bars or test card, they opted for a simple, dark screen with the BBC One logo gently pulsing in the center. Felt like a moment of calm before the storm of the new branding.

**VisualVibes**: I appreciated the subtlety of it—a quiet moment to say goodbye to the old and prepare for the new. Did anyone manage to capture a screenshot? It's one of those blink-and-you'll-miss-it kind of things that hold significance for us branding nerds.

**BroadcastBuddy**: Managed to get a quick pic! It's not the best quality since it was so unexpected, but here's the link: [imaginarylink.jpg]. The pulsing effect created this anticipation, almost like the calm breathing before the day starts. Wonder if that was intentional, symbolizing the 'breath' before the new look.

**LogoLover**: That's really cool, @BroadcastBuddy! Thanks for sharing. The choice of a pulsing logo definitely feels intentional. It evokes a sense of anticipation and heartbeat. A metaphor for the channel itself, staying alive and vibrant through changes.

**RetroWatcher**: Noticed that too. It was a soft farewell to the old idents and a gentle lead-in to the new. I find these moments fascinating - how a simple placeholder or transition can convey so much about a channel's identity and its evolution.

**DesignDiva**: It's moments like these that remind us of the power of visual identity in broadcasting. That placeholder, while minimal, was loaded with meaning. A quiet nod to the past and a soft yet promising beacon of change. Plus, it was a smart way to maintain viewer engagement during a typically 'dead' time.

**NostalgiaNerd**: Absolutely, @DesignDiva. It’s these subtle touches that make brand transitions interesting. It wasn’t just a placeholder; it was a statement—like a palette cleanser before the main course. Made me even more excited for the 6am reveal.

**IdentityInsider**: It's fascinating to think about the thought process that goes into even the smallest details of a brand's visual presentation. That placeholder was a bridge between eras for BBC One. Looking forward to seeing how this attention to detail plays out in the new idents and overall branding.

**ScreenSavvy**: This discussion makes me appreciate the art behind broadcasting even more. Every element, even a temporary placeholder, contains a piece of the channel's story and its journey. Can't wait to dissect the new idents with you all. Here's to fresh beginnings and the little details that make them memorable!

The conversation reveals a shared appreciation for the thoughtful consideration behind transitional moments in broadcast design, highlighting the community's keen interest in the storytelling potential of visual identity elements.

Write a convo asking which version of the logo it was, because the new version has a lowercase "one" placed next to the logo, whereas the outgoing one has One in caps and below the logo, also the lockup is in a red box

**LogoLover**: Quick question for everyone, especially those who caught the placeholder before the new idents aired - which version of the BBC One logo was used? I'm trying to determine if they introduced the new logo styling in that transitional moment. The latest version has a lowercase "one" placed next to the logo instead of the outgoing style with "One" in caps and located below the logo. Plus, the new lockup is reportedly in a red box. Did anyone notice this detail?

**PixelPusher**: Good eye, @LogoLover! Yes, from what I observed during that placeholder moment, it seemed like they were using the new logo configuration. The “one” was indeed in lowercase and positioned next to the BBC logo. However, because of the minimalistic approach and the dark background, I didn't catch the red box. Maybe it was more subtle?

**DesignDiva**: @PixelPusher, I think you're right. I also noticed the lowercase "one" but couldn't make out a red box. It was very minimalist, which might have meant the red box was either too subtle in that context or they opted not to use it during the placeholder screen to keep everything ultra-minimal. 

**BroadcastBuddy**: I'm glad you brought this up! I was specifically looking out for this change. The lowercase "one" was clearly part of the transition. As for the red box, if it was there, it was indeed very subtle. The focus was definitely on the logo's text change, subtly hinting at the new branding direction without giving too much away.

**RetroWatcher**: This change represents a significant shift in branding strategy, doesn’t it? Moving from uppercase to lowercase suggests a more approachable, modern feel. Even without the red box being prominent in the placeholder, adjusting the logo’s text arrangement sets a new tone.

**VisualVibes**: That's true, @RetroWatcher. This shift in logo design reflects a broader trend in branding towards accessibility and informality, which the lowercase "one" encapsulates perfectly. It's interesting that they chose this moment of transition to start introducing the new identity, even in such a subdued manner.

**NostalgiaNerd**: Missed the transition myself, but loving this analysis. The evolution of a channel's logo, especially one as iconic as BBC One, is always fascinating. It's like witnessing history in real-time. The choice of lowercase and the positioning change seem to signify a move towards a more contemporary and casual branding philosophy.

**IdentityInsider**: Exactly, @NostalgiaNerd. It's more than just a logo change; it's about repositioning the channel in the viewers' minds. The lowercase "one" next to the logo, even if the red box wasn't as noticeable, marks a departure from the traditional and signals a fresh, modern outlook. It’s subtle yet impactful.

**ScreenSavvy**: Wow, didn't expect such a small detail to spark such an insightful discussion! Goes to show how integral branding is to a channel's identity and how even the smallest changes can reflect larger strategic shifts. Excited to see how this new logo plays out across all BBC One branding and communication.

**LogoLover**: Thanks for weighing in, everyone! It’s these nuances that make the world of broadcast design so intriguing.
